question_text: The unit of power in the SI system is the watt (W). Which of the following correctly defines one watt?

options.label: A
options.text: One joule of energy transferred in one hour

options.label: B
options.text: One joule of energy transferred in one second

options.label: C
options.text: One kilogram of energy transferred per second

options.label: D
options.text: One joule of energy stored in a system

correct_answer: One joule of energy transferred in one second
explanation: Power is the rate at which energy is transferred or used. One watt is defined as one joule of energy being transferred in one second (1 W = 1 J/s). This is the standard SI unit for measuring power.
